title: T-024 — Wiring: Allergen cascade rule deployment to rule_definitions
prompt: Deploy the `allergen_cascade_rm_to_fg` rule per §10.2 + ADR-029. Create migration adding row in `rule_definitions` (02-SETTINGS §7) with the JSON DSL from §10.2: triggers (bom_lines.insert/update + item_allergen_profiles.update WHERE item_type='rm'), aggregation logic, manufacturing_operation_allergen_additions merge, override protection (preserve source='manual_override'). Implement runtime engine handler in lib/technical/allergens/cascade.ts that the existing rule engine invokes. Per V-TEC-41/45. Async — uses outbox pattern. RED tests/wiring/allergen-cascade.test.ts covers: changing RM allergen propagates to FG within one engine cycle; manual_override row is NOT overwritten. Run: `pnpm vitest run tests/wiring/allergen-cascade.test.ts`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Deploy cascade rule definition + handler; assert RM→intermediate→FG propagation and override protection.
pipeline_inputs.details: Rule row written via Drizzle migration that targets rule_definitions table (created by 02-SETTINGS). Handler executes: SELECT distinct active BOMs containing changed component; aggregate allergens; UPSERT cascaded rows; never overwrite manual_override. End-to-end test seeds a small graph FG -> intermediate -> RM and asserts propagation under <5s.
acceptance_criteria: Given FG has BOM with RM that gets allergen A01 added, when cascade runs, then FG profile contains cascaded A01 within one engine cycle.
Given FG has manual_override row for A01='trace', when RM A01 changes intensity, then FG manual_override row is preserved unchanged.
Given KPI target ≤5s, when measured in test, then propagation completes within 5000ms.
Given manufacturing_operation has allergen_addition for A12, when BOM uses that op, then FG cascaded set includes A12.
test_strategy: RED tests covering propagation + override protection + timing.
risk_red_lines: Do not run cascade synchronously in API request paths.
